H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Protocol H-OS-L1 has n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong, the largest n in HEALING. Qualitative (n=29) shows noticeable improvement for 25, settles around 10 minutes, rhythm balanced and calming. YouTube views=30, the highest reach among all protocols; retention data missing. AutoGluon predicts 6.859 for overstim, aligning with observed mean. It consistently outperforms other HEALING protocols.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: One of the most effective for overstimulation; people liked the music and felt better after about ten minutes, with strong overall reach.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Protocol H-OS-L2 has n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong, with modest spread. Qualitative (n=21) indicates noticeable improvement for 13, settles at ~10 minutes, music neutral for some. YouTube views=5; retention unavailable. AutoGluon drift 0.780 signals significant analytical shift. Outcomes are stable but visibility low.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Effective for sensory overload, with most feeling better, though a few needed longer to settle; reach is limited.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Protocol H-OS-L3 has n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful, relatively tight variance. Qualitative (n=23) shows noticeable improvement for 18, quick 5‑minute settlement, rhythm well‑received. YouTube views=8; retention data missing. AutoGluon predicts 6.859 for overstim, aligning closely. Performance mirrors H-OS-L1 but with smaller reach.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Calms overstimulation well; many settle quickly and enjoy the rhythm, but fewer listeners have tried it.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Protocol H-AX-L1 has n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong, with a biphasic response distribution (14 low, 5 high). Qualitative (n=23) shows moderate-to-significant improvement, 10‑minute settlement for many, and balanced/calming rhythm ratings. YouTube views=7 (2026‑05‑22); retention unavailable. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 efficacy for anxiety, higher than observed, suggesting possible under‑detection. Overall, it aligns with HEALING series expectations of consistent improvement.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most listeners felt noticeably better, especially for anxiety, and settled within about ten minutes, praising the calming rhythm.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Protocol H-AX-L2 has n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful, with relatively tight variance. Qualitative (n=20) reports noticeable improvement for half, quick 5‑minute settlement, and mixed music reception (6 neutral). YouTube views=6; retention data missing. AutoGluon drift score 0.652 indicates evolving framing. It shows solid outcomes but lower reach than H-AX-L1.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Helpful for anxiety relief, with many settling quickly and finding the rhythm soothing, though some felt neutral about the music.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Protocol H-AX-L3 has n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful, with moderate variance and some low scores. Qualitative (n=20) indicates 15/20 improvement, 10‑minute settlement for many, and rhythm rated very calming. YouTube views=4; retention unavailable. AutoGluon drift 0.695 signals analytical shift. It mirrors H-AX-L1 but with smaller n.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Effective for anxiety, though fewer saw major improvement; most settled within ten minutes and liked the calming rhythm.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Protocol H-CR-L1 has n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful, positioned as low‑intensity crisis protocol. Qualitative (n=24) shows 15/24 significant improvement, immediate to 5‑minute settlement, high music liking. YouTube views=6; retention missing. AutoGluon predicts 7.955 efficacy, exceeding observed mean, indicating latent benefit. The pattern fits expected crisis dynamics.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Used in crises, this protocol helped many calm down quickly, with high music liking despite modest mean scores.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Protocol H-CR-L2 has n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful, with moderate variance. Qualitative (n=16) shows 15/16 improvement, 5‑minute settlement, mixed music feedback. H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Good for crisis moments, offering noticeable improvement for half the listeners, though music feedback was mixed.

Qualitative (n=17) shows noticeable improvement for 13, quick settlement for many, rhythm varied. YouTube views=7; retention missing. AutoGluon drift indicates volatile analytical framing. Promising yet volatile outcomes.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Mixed results: some feel uplift quickly, others don’t; high score but large swings suggest limited universal appeal.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Protocol X-MC-L1 has n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful, moderate variance. Qualitative (n=25) shows noticeable improvement for 18, with two reported worsening, music loved. YouTube views=8; retention missing. AutoGluon predicts 7.538 efficacy for mind clarity, higher than observed, indicating latent benefit. Moderate outcomes with adverse reactions flagged.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Clears the mind for many, though a few feel worse; music is widely loved, making it a standout.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Protocol X-MC-L2 has n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing, moderate variance. Qualitative (n=20) shows significant improvement for 7, immediate settlement for 6, music loved. YouTube views=12; retention unavailable. AutoGluon predicts 7.538, aligning closely, but small n limits certainty. Strong qualitative response despite limited data.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Fast‑acting and calming for mental clarity; loved by self‑listeners, though data remains limited.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Protocol X-MC-L3 has n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, confidence=developing, lowest mean and highest variance across all protocols. Qualitative (n=17) shows noticeable improvement for 9, but 5 rated rhythm too busy, two scored 1‑2. YouTube views=8; retention missing. AutoGluon predicts lower efficacy than H-CR-L2, raising concern for usability. Significant redesign needed.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Struggles to connect; some dislike the rhythm or feel no change, indicating a need for redesign.

sphere_commentary.overview:
Across all protocols, mean outcome scores cluster between 6.5 and 7.6, with HEALING showing the highest consistency, THRIVING achieving the strongest means, and TRANSFORMING exhibiting the widest variability.
• HEALING protocols achieve strong confidence and low variance, indicating reliable listener improvement.
• THRIVING reaches the highest mean scores, particularly Summit (T-FC-L3) and Aurora (T-CF-L3).
• TRANSFORMING shows substantial spread, especially in X-MC-L3 and X-MD-L1, suggesting subgroup effects.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Each series aligns with its design intent, yet reveals distinct efficacy patterns.
• HEALING demonstrates low variance and dominant confidence, especially in over‑stimulation and anxiety protocols.
• THRIVING features the highest mean outcomes, with Summit (T-FC-L3) leading at 7.63.
• TRANSFORMING exhibits lower means and higher variance, reflecting its ascending‑arc and crisis design.
• The distribution of confidence tiers mirrors series size, with early data remaining less certain.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocols deviate from expected patterns, highlighting data instability or listener heterogeneity.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45) records the lowest score and highest variance.
• X-MD-L1 (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01) has the lowest pct_pos (45%) across all protocols.
• X-MD-L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38) shows the highest analytical drift (sim=0.507).
• H-CR-L3 (n=27, std=1.91) exhibits wide response spread despite moderate mean.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements can address identified inefficiencies and latent signals.
• X-MC-L3 — [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Pause routing; redesign with subtler arc and adjusted Hz/duration.
• X-MD-L3 — [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Extend ease‑in and adjust ascent parameters to improve entrainment.
• X-ME-L1 — [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Expand cross‑bucket routing; treat as universal entry point for low‑affect states.
• T-FC-L3 — [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Optimize hold phase; replicate its design elements in similar THRIVING protocols.
• T-MB-L3 — [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Reduce non‑settle responses; adjust entrainment curve.
• H-CR-L3 — Investigate bimodal crisis response; refine adviser routing and substrate variation.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Drift and clustering indicate evolving analytical framing and limited semantic patterns.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: all 30 protocols show similarity below 0.85, with X‑MD‑L3 at 0.507 as the most divergent.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: insufficient data prevents robust clusters; only HEALING and THRIVING groups emerge clearly.
• Confidence note: limited run history and widespread drift raise uncertainty about stable analytical categories.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Most listeners felt noticeably better, especially for anxiety, and settled within about ten minutes, praising the calming rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Helpful for anxiety relief, with many settling quickly and finding the rhythm soothing, though some felt neutral about the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Effective for anxiety, though fewer saw major improvement; most settled within ten minutes and liked the calming rhythm.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Used in crises, this protocol helped many calm down quickly, with high music liking despite modest mean scores.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Good for crisis moments, offering noticeable improvement for half the listeners, though music feedback was mixed.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Helps some feel better fast in crises, but a few find the rhythm too busy, leading to varied results.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: One of the most effective for overstimulation; people liked the music and felt better after about ten minutes, with strong overall reach.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Effective for sensory overload, with most feeling better, though a few needed longer to settle; reach is limited.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Calms overstimulation well; many settle quickly and enjoy the rhythm, but fewer listeners have tried it.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Helps many wind down and fall asleep, loved the music and settled quickly, though few have viewed it.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Used by caregivers and clinicians, some felt improvement but others found the rhythm busy; reach is low.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Gentle for relaxation and sleep prep, most felt better, though a few found the rhythm slightly busy; small audience.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Supports creative flow for many, especially those already calm, with quick settling and liked music, though effects are subtle.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Loved by small groups, especially in creative settings, with noticeable shifts and strong music appreciation.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Promising for creativity; many report big improvements, though few have tried it and results are still emerging.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Helps with focus, though some find the rhythm a bit distracting; useful but not the strongest in its category.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Supports staying on task for many, even if rhythm feels busy to some; moderate improvement across listeners.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: One of the best for focus; many feel significantly sharper and love the music, gaining increasing attention.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Balancing and steady, but takes longer to settle; best for longer sessions where slow entrainment is acceptable.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Mixed reactions — some find it helpful, others feel the rhythm distracting — consistency is low.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Helpful for balance for many, though some don’t settle at all; more data needed to trust the results.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Clears the mind for many, though a few feel worse; music is widely loved, making it a standout.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Fast‑acting and calming for mental clarity; loved by self‑listeners, though data remains limited.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Struggles to connect; some dislike the rhythm or feel no change, indicating a need for redesign.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Aims to boost motivation, but responses split — some energize, others see no change, showing wide variation.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Too slow to take effect for some — settling takes up to 20 minutes — raises questions about suitability for urgent needs.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Mixed results: some feel uplift quickly, others don’t; high score but large swings suggest limited universal appeal.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Top choice for low energy; many feel meaningfully better, even if it takes 20 minutes to settle.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Calming or energizing for many, though settling times vary widely, creating a mixed experience.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Shows modest improvement for many but also some no‑effect reports, suggesting refinements needed.
